user_query: I need a small, verifiable porting assessment based on the local inputs and a concise PM-ready update. Please: 1) Read input/windows_api_usage.json (Windows API call counts), input/api_mapping.csv (Windowsâ†’macOS mappings with status), and input/macos_docs.html (offline macOS API availability table). 2) Extract structured availability from macos_docs.html by parsing the table with id="availability" into rows: name, availability (available|deprecated|unavailable), note. Treat availability as normalized_doc_status where availableâ†’exists, deprecatedâ†’partial, unavailableâ†’none. 3) For each Windows API in windows_api_usage.json, join to api_mapping.csv (if present) and then to macos_docs.html by macos_equivalent name; if a Windows API has no mapping, mark mapping_status="unmapped", macos_equivalent=null, doc_availability=null, normalized_doc_status="none". 4) Compute weighted_coverage = sum(calls Ã— weight)/sum(calls), with weight(exists)=1, weight(partial)=0.5, weight(none)=0. 5) Compute priority_score = calls Ã— (1 âˆ’ weight_from_normalized_doc_status). 6) Produce outputs/porting_report.json with at least: { "totals": { "distinct_windows_apis": int, "total_calls": int }, "weighted_coverage": float, "by_api": [ { "windows_api": string, "calls": int, "macos_equivalent": string|null, "mapping_status": "exists|partial|none|unmapped", "doc_availability": "available|deprecated|unavailable|null", "normalized_doc_status": "exists|partial|none|unknown", "priority_score": float } ], "mapping_discrepancies": [ { "windows_api": string, "macos_equivalent": string, "mapping_status": string, "doc_availability": string } ], "missing_mappings": [string] }. Include a discrepancy whenever mapping_status does not match normalized_doc_status derived from docs. 7) Produce outputs/priority.csv sorted by priority_score desc with columns: windows_api,calls,macos_equivalent,mapping_status,doc_availability,priority_score. 8) Rewrite input/dev_update.txt into outputs/update_message.txt: a concise (80â€“130 words), professional, neutral-toned status for PMs that includes the computed weighted_coverage as a percentage with one decimal, and names the top 3 Windows APIs by priority_score with their scores. Avoid internal jargon, keep it actionable and clear. Do not hardcode numbers; base everything on the provided inputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
